concept.name: Apparent Power
concept.description: Total apparent power measured in kilovolt-amperes (kVA). Represents the combined real and reactive power.

notes: Apparent power = sqrt(real power² + reactive power²)
Used for sizing transformers, generators, and UPS systems

concept.name: Boiler Alarm
concept.description: Boiler alarm point indicating fault or warning condition

concept.name: Boiler Pump Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from boiler pump

concept.name: Building Pressure Setpoint
concept.description: Target differential pressure between the building interior and outdoors. Positive pressure prevents unconditioned air infiltration.

notes: Typical setpoint is +0.03 to +0.10 inWC (positive relative to outdoors)
Controlled by modulating relief/exhaust air dampers or fans
Negative building pressure causes comfort complaints from cold drafts and door operation issues

concept.name: Bypass Valve Closed
concept.description: Binary status indicating if bypass valve is closed

concept.name: Bypass Valve Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from bypass valve

concept.name: Bypass Valve Open
concept.description: Binary status indicating if bypass valve is open

concept.name: Chilled Water Differential Pressure Setpoint
concept.description: Target differential pressure across the chilled water loop, used to control chilled water pump speed. Ensures adequate flow to all coils.

notes: Controls variable speed pump operation to maintain adequate pressure across the most remote coil
ASHRAE Guideline 36 recommends differential pressure reset based on valve positions
Sensor should be located at the most hydraulically remote coil, not at the pump discharge
Typical range is 5-25 PSI depending on system size

concept.name: Chilled Water Flow
concept.description: Volumetric flow rate of chilled water through a coil, pipe, or system, measured in gallons per minute (GPM) or liters per second. Used for load calculations and delta-T monitoring.

notes: Used with supply/return temperature differential to calculate cooling load (BTU = GPM × 500 × ΔT)
Flow meters may be electromagnetic, ultrasonic, or differential pressure type
Low flow with high delta-T may indicate valve or pump issues

concept.name: Chilled Water Pump Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from chilled water pump

concept.name: Chilled Water Supply Temperature
concept.description: Temperature of chilled water leaving the chiller plant

notes: Typical setpoint 42-44°F for comfort cooling
Higher setpoints (50-55°F) for energy optimization
Must stay above 40°F to prevent coil freezing
Delta T from return typically 10-14°F

concept.name: Chilled Water Supply Temperature Setpoint
concept.description: Target supply temperature for chilled water leaving the chiller or chilled water plant. Fundamental to chiller control and energy optimization.

notes: Typical range is 40-48°F (4-9°C)
Higher setpoints improve chiller efficiency (approximately 1-2% per degree F)
ASHRAE Guideline 36 recommends CHW supply temperature reset based on valve positions or outdoor air temperature

concept.name: Chilled Water Valve Closed
concept.description: Binary status indicating if chilled water valve is closed

concept.name: Chilled Water Valve Open
concept.description: Binary status indicating if chilled water valve is open

concept.name: Chiller Alarm
concept.description: Chiller alarm point indicating fault or warning condition

concept.name: Chiller Entering Temperature
concept.description: Chiller entering water temperature (return water temperature entering chiller)

concept.name: Chiller Leaving Temperature
concept.description: Chiller leaving water temperature (supply water temperature leaving chiller)

concept.name: CO2 Setpoint
concept.description: Target CO2 concentration for demand-controlled ventilation. When zone CO2 exceeds this setpoint, outdoor air damper opens to increase ventilation.

notes: Typical values range from 800-1100 ppm depending on space type
ASHRAE 62.1 does not specify a fixed CO2 setpoint — it depends on outdoor CO2 and occupant density
Lower setpoints increase ventilation energy but improve IAQ

concept.name: Communication Loss Alarm
concept.description: Alarm indicating loss of communication between a BAS controller and a supervised device or sensor. May indicate network failure, device failure, or wiring issue.

notes: One of the most common alarms in any BAS
May be caused by wiring issues, power loss at the device, or network switch failure
Should trigger investigation within hours to prevent uncontrolled equipment operation

concept.name: Condenser Water Flow
concept.description: Volumetric flow rate of condenser water between chillers and cooling towers. Critical for maintaining proper chiller condenser performance.

notes: Chiller manufacturers specify minimum condenser water flow rates
Low condenser water flow causes high head pressure and potential chiller trip
Typically 3 GPM per ton of cooling

concept.name: Condenser Water Pump Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from condenser water pump

concept.name: Condenser Water Supply Temperature Setpoint
concept.description: Target condenser water temperature leaving the cooling tower and entering the chiller condenser. Controls cooling tower fan speed.

notes: Typical range is 70-85°F (21-29°C) depending on weather and chiller requirements
Lower condenser water temperature improves chiller efficiency but increases tower fan energy
Optimal setpoint balances chiller and tower energy (often calculated via chiller COP curves)
Minimum is limited by chiller manufacturer low-condenser-water cutout (typically 55-65°F)

concept.name: Condenser Water Valve Closed
concept.description: Binary status indicating if condenser water valve is closed

concept.name: Condenser Water Valve Open
concept.description: Binary status indicating if condenser water valve is open

concept.name: Cooling Outdoor Air Lockout Setpoint
concept.description: Cooling Outdoor Air Lockout Setpoint point
concept.haystack: outside air temp cooling lockout sp

concept.name: Cooling Tower Alarm
concept.description: Cooling tower alarm point indicating fault or warning condition

concept.name: Cooling Tower Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from cooling tower

concept.name: Cooling Tower Isolation Valve
concept.description: Cooling tower isolation valve position or command

concept.name: Cooling Tower Output
concept.description: Cooling tower fan speed output signal (0-100%)

concept.name: Cooling Tower Status
concept.description: Cooling tower running status feedback point

concept.name: Cooling Valve Closed
concept.description: Binary status indicating if cooling valve is closed

concept.name: Cooling Valve Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from cooling valve

concept.name: Cooling Valve Open
concept.description: Binary status indicating if cooling valve is open

concept.name: Daylight Illuminance
concept.description: Measured natural daylight intensity at a sensor location, in lux. Used for daylight harvesting to reduce electric lighting usage.

notes: Typical office target is 300-500 lux (30-50 fc) at the task plane
Sensor placement impacts accuracy — avoid direct sunlight on the sensor
Used to modulate electric lighting in daylight harvesting sequences

concept.name: Dew Point Temperature
concept.description: Temperature at which air becomes saturated and condensation begins. Critical for dehumidification control, condensation prevention, and cooling coil analysis.

notes: More stable than relative humidity for dehumidification control (not affected by temperature changes)
Typical comfort range is 55-65°F (13-18°C) dew point
Critical in healthcare, museums, and data centers
Can be calculated from dry bulb temperature and relative humidity

concept.name: Discharge Air High Pressure Alarm
concept.description: Discharge Air High Pressure Alarm point

concept.name: Discharge Air Low Pressure Alarm
concept.description: Discharge Air Low Pressure Alarm point

concept.name: Discharge Air Pressure
concept.description: Static pressure in supply air duct, used to control fan speed

notes: Typically measured 2/3 down the longest duct run
Used as feedback for supply fan VFD speed control
Setpoint typically 1.0-2.0 in.wc for VAV systems
Higher setpoint needed for longer duct runs

concept.name: Discharge Air Temperature
concept.description: Temperature of air leaving the air handling unit or terminal unit

notes: Cooling mode typically 50-65°F
Heating mode typically 70-110°F
Sometimes called Supply Air Temperature (SAT) downstream of unit
Critical for discharge air temperature control loops

concept.name: Domestic Hot Water Return Temperature
concept.description: Domestic Hot Water Return Temperature point
concept.haystack: domestic hot water entering temp sensor

concept.name: Domestic Hot Water Supply Temperature
concept.description: Domestic Hot Water Supply Temperature point
concept.haystack: domestic hot water leaving temp sensor

concept.name: Duct Static Pressure
concept.description: Static pressure measured in the supply air duct, typically 2/3 of the way down the longest duct run. Primary control input for supply fan speed in VAV systems.

notes: Typical setpoint for VAV systems is 1.0-2.0 inWC
ASHRAE Guideline 36 recommends static pressure reset based on VAV box damper positions
Sensor location at 2/3 point of the longest duct run is the industry standard
Low duct static with fan at full speed may indicate duct leakage or obstruction

concept.name: Duct Static Pressure Setpoint
concept.description: Target static pressure in the supply duct. Primary setpoint for supply fan speed control in VAV systems.

notes: Typical initial setpoint is 1.0-2.0 inWC for medium-pressure ductwork
ASHRAE Guideline 36 recommends static pressure reset — lower setpoint when VAV boxes are satisfied, raise when boxes are starved
Static pressure reset can save 20-40% of fan energy

concept.name: DX Cooling Stage
concept.description: Direct expansion cooling stage status point

concept.name: Effective Cooling Setpoint
concept.haystack: zone air temp effective cooling sp

concept.name: Effective Discharge Air Temperature Setpoint
concept.description: Effective Discharge Air Temperature Setpoint point

concept.name: Effective Heating Setpoint
concept.haystack: zone air temp effective heating sp

concept.name: Electrical Current
concept.description: AC current draw measured in amperes. Used for load monitoring, motor protection, and energy calculations.

notes: Often measured via current transformers (CTs) on each phase
Rising current on a motor can indicate bearing failure or overload
Used with voltage for calculating power (P = V × I × PF)

concept.name: Electrical Demand
concept.description: Instantaneous electrical power draw measured in kilowatts. Used for demand monitoring, load shedding, and utility demand charge management.

notes: Measures real (active) power consumption
Used for utility demand charge calculations and load shedding programs
Typically read from a power meter or CT (current transformer) metering

concept.name: Energy Consumption
concept.description: Cumulative electrical energy consumption measured in kilowatt-hours. Used for energy tracking, utility billing verification, and energy benchmarking.

notes: Cumulative counter that rolls over or resets based on meter configuration
Used for utility bill verification and energy benchmarking (e.g., EUI calculations)
Often tracked per equipment, floor, or building

concept.name: Equipment Run Hours
concept.description: Cumulative operating hours for a piece of equipment. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling based on runtime thresholds.

notes: Typically tracked per motor, compressor, or fan
Common maintenance thresholds include belt replacement (2000-4000 hrs), bearing lubrication (8000 hrs), and filter changes (based on differential pressure OR run hours)
Differs from run-time (existing entry) which may track current session

concept.name: Equipment Start Count
concept.description: Cumulative number of equipment start cycles. Excessive cycling reduces equipment life, especially for compressors and large motors.

notes: Motor starters and compressors have rated cycle limits
Compressor minimum off-time (typically 5 minutes) prevents short cycling
High start count relative to run hours indicates a control tuning problem

concept.name: Exhaust Air Damper Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from exhaust air damper

concept.name: Exhaust Fan Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from exhaust fan

concept.name: Face Bypass Damper Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from face bypass damper

concept.name: Filter Differential Pressure
concept.description: Pressure drop measured across an air filter. Rising differential pressure indicates filter loading and the need for replacement.

notes: Clean filter DP is typically 0.1-0.3 inWC for MERV 8, 0.3-0.6 inWC for MERV 13
Filter change is typically triggered at 1.0-1.5 inWC depending on filter type
More reliable than timer-based filter changes
Should be measured with a differential pressure transducer across the filter bank

concept.name: Fire Alarm Status
concept.description: Fire alarm system general alarm status. When active, BAS systems typically execute smoke control sequences including fan shutdown, damper closure, and elevator recall.

notes: This is a monitoring point from the fire alarm panel — the BAS does NOT control fire alarm systems
Triggers smoke control sequences defined by the fire protection engineer
Typically a dry contact input from the fire alarm control panel (FACP)
All smoke dampers close and fans shut down (or enter smoke evacuation mode) on fire alarm

concept.name: AC Frequency
concept.description: AC power system frequency in Hertz. Monitors grid stability and generator synchronization.

notes: Standard is 60 Hz (North America) or 50 Hz (Europe/Asia)
Deviations from nominal indicate grid stress or generator issues
Critical for generator synchronization and UPS systems

concept.name: Gas Fired Heat Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from gas fired heat

concept.name: Heat Wheel Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from heat wheel

concept.name: Heating Outdoor Air Lockout Setpoint
concept.description: Heating Outdoor Air Lockout Setpoint point
concept.haystack: outside air temp heating lockout sp

concept.name: Heating Valve Closed
concept.description: Binary status indicating if heating valve is closed

concept.name: Heating Valve Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from heating valve

concept.name: Heating Valve Open
concept.description: Binary status indicating if heating valve is open

concept.name: Hot Water Flow
concept.description: Volumetric flow rate of hot water through a coil, pipe, or system. Used for heating load calculations.

notes: Used with supply/return temperature differential to calculate heating load
Similar instrumentation to chilled water flow

concept.name: Hot Water Pump Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from hot water pump

concept.name: Hot Water Supply Temperature Setpoint
concept.description: Target supply temperature for hot water leaving the boiler or hot water plant. Controls boiler firing and hot water system operation.

notes: Typical range is 140-180°F (60-82°C)
Lower setpoints reduce boiler losses and improve condensing boiler efficiency
Often reset based on outdoor air temperature (outdoor air reset schedule)

concept.name: Hot Water Valve Closed
concept.description: Binary status indicating if hot water valve is closed

concept.name: Hot Water Valve Open
concept.description: Binary status indicating if hot water valve is open

concept.name: Zone Humidity Setpoint
concept.description: Target relative humidity for a conditioned zone. Controls humidification and dehumidification equipment.

notes: Typical comfort range is 30-60% RH
Healthcare facilities often require tighter control (40-60% RH)
Data centers typically target 40-60% RH per ASHRAE TC 9.9
Some systems use separate high-limit and low-limit humidity setpoints

concept.name: Isolation Valve Closed
concept.description: Binary status indicating if isolation valve is closed

concept.name: Isolation Valve Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from isolation valve

concept.name: Isolation Valve Open
concept.description: Binary status indicating if isolation valve is open

concept.name: Lighting Dimming Output
concept.description: Commanded dimming level sent to lighting ballasts or LED drivers, expressed as a percentage (0-100%).

notes: Typically controlled via 0-10V analog, DALI, or DMX protocol
May be overridden by occupancy sensor or daylight harvesting logic

concept.name: Lighting Level
concept.description: Measured or commanded light intensity level, typically as a percentage (0-100%) of full output. Used for dimming control and daylight harvesting.

notes: 0% = off, 100% = full brightness
Some systems report in lux (absolute illumination) rather than percentage
Daylight harvesting systems modulate lighting level based on natural light availability

concept.name: Lighting Occupancy Sensor
concept.description: Presence detection sensor for lighting control. Detects whether a space is occupied to enable or disable lighting.

notes: PIR (passive infrared), ultrasonic, and dual-technology types available
Vacancy sensors require manual ON, auto OFF — preferred by energy codes
Occupancy sensors auto ON, auto OFF
Timeout period is configurable (typically 15-30 minutes)

concept.name: Minimum Outdoor Airflow Setpoint
concept.description: Minimum volume of outdoor air required for ventilation per ASHRAE 62.1. Ensures adequate ventilation regardless of economizer mode.

notes: Calculated per ASHRAE 62.1 based on occupancy, floor area, and space type
Must be maintained even when economizer is not active
In DCV mode, this setpoint may vary with occupancy count or CO2 levels

concept.name: Mixed Air Damper Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from mixed air damper

concept.name: Mixing Valve Closed
concept.description: Binary status indicating if mixing valve is closed

concept.name: Mixing Valve Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from mixing valve

concept.name: Mixing Valve Open
concept.description: Binary status indicating if mixing valve is open

concept.name: Occupied Cooling Setpoint
concept.haystack: zone air temp occ cooling sp

concept.name: Occupied Heating Setpoint
concept.haystack: zone air temp occ heating sp

concept.name: Outdoor Air CO2 Concentration
concept.description: Outdoor ambient CO2 level used as a baseline reference for demand-controlled ventilation calculations.

notes: Typically 400-420 ppm in most locations
Used as baseline for differential CO2 DCV calculations
A single outdoor sensor can serve multiple AHUs

concept.name: Outdoor Air Temperature
concept.description: Temperature of outside ambient air, used for economizer and lockout control

notes: Critical for economizer enable/disable decisions
Used for heating/cooling lockout control
Often shared across multiple AHUs via BACnet
May be provided by weather service in some systems

concept.name: Outside Air Damper Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from outside air damper

concept.name: PM10 Particulate Matter
concept.description: Particulate matter with diameter ≤10 microns, measured in micrograms per cubic meter. Includes dust, pollen, and mold spores.

notes: Coarser than PM2.5, includes dust, pollen, and mold spores
Less commonly monitored than PM2.5 in commercial HVAC, but relevant for industrial and healthcare facilities

concept.name: PM2.5 Particulate Matter
concept.description: Fine particulate matter with diameter ≤2.5 microns, measured in micrograms per cubic meter. Critical for indoor air quality assessment, especially post-COVID building standards.

notes: EPA Air Quality Index (AQI) categories for PM2.5 range from 0 (good) to 500+ (hazardous)
Increasingly required in commercial buildings for IAQ monitoring
Used to control filtration levels and outdoor air intake during poor outdoor air quality events

concept.name: Power Factor
concept.description: Ratio of real power to apparent power, ranging from 0.0 to 1.0. Indicates how effectively electrical power is being used.

notes: A power factor of 1.0 means all power is real (resistive loads)
Most utilities penalize power factor below 0.85-0.90
Can be improved with capacitor banks or VFDs

concept.name: Primary Chilled Water Pump Alarm
concept.description: Primary Chilled Water Pump Alarm point
concept.haystack: primaryLoop chilled water pump alarm sensor

concept.name: Primary Chilled Water Pump Command
concept.description: Primary Chilled Water Pump Command point
concept.haystack: primaryLoop chilled water pump run cmd

concept.name: Primary Chilled Water Pump Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from primary chilled water pump
concept.haystack: primaryLoop chilled water pump speed sensor

concept.name: Primary Chilled Water Pump Output
concept.description: Primary Chilled Water Pump Output point
concept.haystack: primaryLoop chilled water pump speed cmd

concept.name: Primary Chilled Water Pump Status
concept.description: Primary Chilled Water Pump Status point
concept.haystack: primaryLoop chilled water pump run sensor

concept.name: Primary Chilled Water Return Temperature
concept.description: Primary Chilled Water Return Temperature point
concept.haystack: primaryLoop chilled water entering temp sensor

concept.name: Primary Chilled Water Supply Temperature
concept.description: Primary Chilled Water Supply Temperature point
concept.haystack: primaryLoop chilled water leaving temp sensor

concept.name: Primary Hot Water Return Temperature
concept.description: Primary Hot Water Return Temperature point
concept.haystack: primaryLoop hot water entering temp sensor

concept.name: Primary Hot Water Supply Temperature
concept.description: Primary Hot Water Supply Temperature point
concept.haystack: primaryLoop hot water leaving temp sensor

concept.name: Reactive Power
concept.description: Reactive power measured in kilovolt-amperes reactive (kVAR). Indicates inductive or capacitive load contribution.

notes: High reactive power reduces power factor and may incur utility penalties
Common sources include motors, transformers, and fluorescent lighting ballasts

concept.name: Relief Air Damper Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from relief air damper

concept.name: Relief Fan Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from relief fan

concept.name: Return Air High Pressure Alarm
concept.description: Return Air High Pressure Alarm point

concept.name: Return Air Low Pressure Alarm
concept.description: Return Air Low Pressure Alarm point

concept.name: Return Fan Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from return fan

concept.name: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Alarm
concept.description: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Alarm point
concept.haystack: secondaryLoop chilled water pump alarm sensor

concept.name: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Command
concept.description: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Command point
concept.haystack: secondaryLoop chilled water pump run cmd

concept.name: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from secondary chilled water pump
concept.haystack: secondaryLoop chilled water pump speed sensor

concept.name: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Output
concept.description: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Output point
concept.haystack: secondaryLoop chilled water pump speed cmd

concept.name: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Status
concept.description: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Status point
concept.haystack: secondaryLoop chilled water pump run sensor

concept.name: Secondary Chilled Water Return Temperature
concept.description: Secondary Chilled Water Return Temperature point
concept.haystack: secondaryLoop chilled water entering temp sensor

concept.name: Secondary Chilled Water Supply Temperature
concept.description: Secondary Chilled Water Supply Temperature point
concept.haystack: secondaryLoop chilled water leaving temp sensor

concept.name: Secondary Hot Water Return Temperature
concept.description: Secondary Hot Water Return Temperature point
concept.haystack: secondaryLoop hot water entering temp sensor

concept.name: Secondary Hot Water Supply Temperature
concept.description: Secondary Hot Water Supply Temperature point
concept.haystack: secondaryLoop hot water leaving temp sensor

concept.name: Sensor Fault Alarm
concept.description: Alarm indicating a sensor is reading outside its expected range or has failed. May indicate a broken sensor, wiring issue, or incorrect calibration.

notes: Typically triggered when a sensor reads at the extreme of its range (e.g., thermocouple reads -40°F or +300°F continuously)
Open or short circuit on an analog input triggers this alarm
Control systems should have fallback logic when a sensor faults

concept.name: Standby Cooling Setpoint
concept.haystack: zone air temp standby cooling sp

concept.name: Standby Heating Setpoint
concept.haystack: zone air temp standby heating sp

concept.name: Steam Flow
concept.description: Mass flow rate of steam, typically measured in pounds per hour (lbs/hr) or kilograms per hour. Used for steam system monitoring and energy accounting.

notes: Often measured with vortex shedding, orifice plate, or differential pressure flow meters
Must compensate for steam pressure and temperature for accurate mass flow
Used for campus steam billing and boiler plant monitoring

concept.name: Steam Heating Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from steam heating

concept.name: Steam Valve Closed
concept.description: Binary status indicating if steam valve is closed

concept.name: Steam Valve Open
concept.description: Binary status indicating if steam valve is open

concept.name: Supply Fan Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from supply fan

concept.name: Supply Fan Status
concept.description: Binary status indicating if supply fan is running

notes: Typically from current sensing relay or differential pressure switch
Used for fan failure alarms and interlock logic
Should match command within reasonable delay
Critical for safety interlocks

concept.name: Unoccupied Cooling Setpoint
concept.haystack: zone air temp unocc cooling sp

concept.name: Unoccupied Heating Setpoint
concept.haystack: zone air temp unocc heating sp

concept.name: Volatile Organic Compounds (VOC)
concept.description: Total volatile organic compound concentration. VOCs include chemicals from paints, cleaning products, furnishings, and building materials that impact indoor air quality.

notes: TVOC sensors measure a broad range of organic chemicals as a single reading
WELL Building Standard and RESET Air Standard both specify TVOC thresholds
Can trigger increased ventilation or filtration when levels are elevated

concept.name: Voltage
concept.description: AC voltage measurement, either line-to-neutral or line-to-line. Used for power quality monitoring and equipment protection.

notes: Typical values are 120V, 208V, 277V, 480V (in North America)
Voltage sags/swells can damage equipment and should trigger alarms
Often measured per phase (A, B, C) on three-phase systems

concept.name: Wet Bulb Temperature
concept.description: Temperature measured by a thermometer with its bulb wrapped in wet cloth over which air is passed. Indicates the lowest temperature achievable by evaporative cooling and is critical for cooling tower performance evaluation.

notes: Cooling tower approach = cold water temperature - wet bulb temperature
Used to evaluate cooling tower performance and sizing
Lower wet bulb temperatures allow towers to produce colder condenser water
Critical for free cooling / waterside economizer calculations

concept.name: Zone CO2 Concentration
concept.description: Carbon dioxide concentration measured in a conditioned zone, in parts per million (ppm). Used for demand-controlled ventilation (DCV) per ASHRAE 62.1.

notes: Outdoor ambient CO2 is typically 400-420 ppm
ASHRAE 62.1 DCV typically targets 800-1000 ppm above outdoor baseline
Rising CO2 indicates insufficient ventilation for occupancy level
Sensor placement is critical — avoid near doors, diffusers, or exhaust

concept.name: Zone Temperature
concept.description: Sensed temperature of air in occupied zone or space

notes: Often abbreviated as ZAT (Zone Air Temp) or RAT (Room Air Temp)
In VRF systems, may be called Indoor Unit Temp or FCU Temp
Wireless sensors often use vendor-specific names like EnOcean_Temp_1
When multiple sensors in zone, often suffixed ZoneTemp_1, ZoneTemp_Avg

equipment.name: Air Handling Unit
equipment.description: Central air conditioning unit that conditions and circulates air through ductwork

equipment.name: Air Separator
equipment.description: Device that removes entrained air from hydronic systems to prevent air locks

equipment.name: Air Source Heat Pump
equipment.description: Heat pump that exchanges heat with outdoor air for heating and cooling

equipment.name: Air Turnover Unit
equipment.description: High-volume unit for warehouses and large spaces that destratifies air and provides heating

equipment.name: Automatic Transfer Switch
equipment.description: Switch that automatically transfers electrical load between normal (utility) and emergency (generator) power sources during an outage.

equipment.name: Baseboard Heater
equipment.description: Wall-mounted heating element at floor level, using hot water or electric resistance

equipment.name: Blower Coil Unit
equipment.description: Horizontal or vertical unit with blower and coil, similar to FCU but typically ductless

equipment.name: Boiler
equipment.description: Equipment that heats water or produces steam using gas, oil, or electric heat

equipment.name: BTU Meter
equipment.description: Device that measures thermal energy by calculating flow rate and temperature differential

equipment.name: Chilled Beam
equipment.description: Ceiling-mounted unit using chilled water coils for cooling via convection and radiation

equipment.name: Chiller
equipment.description: Equipment that removes heat from liquid via refrigeration cycle to produce chilled water

equipment.name: Computer Room Air Conditioner
equipment.description: Precision cooling unit designed for data centers and computer rooms with tight temperature control

equipment.name: Computer Room Air Handler
equipment.description: Precision air handler for data centers using chilled water instead of DX cooling

equipment.name: Condensing Unit
equipment.description: Outdoor unit containing compressor(s) and condenser coil for DX (direct expansion) cooling systems. Paired with indoor evaporator coils in split system configurations.

equipment.name: Constant Air Volume Box
equipment.description: Terminal unit that maintains constant airflow, temperature controlled by varying supply air temp

equipment.name: Cooling Tower
equipment.description: Heat rejection device that cools water by evaporation for condenser water systems

equipment.name: Dedicated Outdoor Air System
equipment.description: System that conditions 100% outdoor air for ventilation, separate from space conditioning

equipment.name: Dehumidifier
equipment.description: Standalone equipment for removing moisture from air. Used in spaces with high humidity loads such as pools, locker rooms, and archival storage.

equipment.name: Domestic Water Heater
equipment.description: Equipment that heats domestic (potable) water for building use. May be electric, gas-fired, or use a heat exchanger fed by the building hot water loop.

equipment.name: Domestic Water Recirculation Pump
equipment.description: Pump that circulates domestic hot water through the building to maintain hot water availability at fixtures and prevent Legionella growth in stagnant piping.

equipment.name: Ductless Mini-Split
equipment.description: Ductless split-system HVAC unit consisting of an indoor wall-mounted or ceiling unit connected to an outdoor condensing unit via refrigerant piping. Provides heating and/or cooling without ductwork.

equipment.name: Electric Meter
equipment.description: Device that measures electrical energy consumption in kWh or demand in kW

equipment.name: Energy Recovery Ventilator
equipment.description: Ventilation unit that transfers both heat and moisture between exhaust and supply air

equipment.name: Exhaust Fan
equipment.description: Standalone fan used to exhaust air from a building or space. Commonly found in restrooms, kitchens, parking garages, and mechanical rooms. Not part of an air handling unit.

equipment.name: Expansion Tank
equipment.description: Tank that absorbs expansion of heated water and maintains system pressure

equipment.name: Fan Coil Unit
equipment.description: Small terminal unit with fan and coil for local heating/cooling, typically without ductwork

equipment.name: Fire Alarm Control Panel
equipment.description: Central panel of the fire alarm system. The BAS monitors the FACP for general alarm, trouble, and supervisory conditions to execute smoke control and HVAC shutdown sequences.

equipment.name: Emergency Generator
equipment.description: Standby or emergency power generator, typically diesel or natural gas fueled. Provides backup power during utility outages. Monitored by BAS for runtime, fuel level, and transfer status.

equipment.name: Heat Exchanger
equipment.description: Device that transfers heat between two fluids without mixing them

equipment.name: Heat Recovery Ventilator
equipment.description: Ventilation unit that transfers sensible heat only between exhaust and supply air

equipment.name: Humidifier
equipment.description: Standalone or duct-mounted equipment for adding moisture to air. Common types include steam injection, evaporative, and ultrasonic.

equipment.name: Induction Unit
equipment.description: Terminal unit that uses high-velocity primary air to induce room air across a coil

equipment.name: Makeup Air Unit
equipment.description: Unit that conditions and supplies fresh outdoor air to replace exhausted air

equipment.name: Natural Gas Meter
equipment.description: Device that measures natural gas consumption, typically in cubic feet or therms

equipment.name: Packaged Terminal Air Conditioner
equipment.description: Through-wall self-contained unit for heating and cooling individual rooms, common in hotels

equipment.name: Packaged Terminal Heat Pump
equipment.description: Through-wall heat pump unit providing both heating and cooling for individual rooms

equipment.name: Parallel Fan Powered Box
equipment.description: VAV box with parallel fan that draws plenum air, fan runs intermittently during heating

equipment.name: Pump
equipment.description: Equipment that moves water through hydronic heating or cooling systems

equipment.name: Radiant Panel
equipment.description: Panel system using radiant heat transfer for heating or cooling, ceiling or floor mounted

equipment.name: Rooftop Unit
equipment.description: Self-contained HVAC unit installed on building roof, combines heating, cooling, and ventilation

equipment.name: Series Fan Powered Box
equipment.description: VAV box with series fan that runs continuously, mixing primary and plenum air

equipment.name: Smoke Detector
equipment.description: Device that detects smoke and reports to the fire alarm control panel. Duct-mounted smoke detectors are monitored by the BAS to shut down associated air handling units.

equipment.name: Steam Meter
equipment.description: Device that measures steam flow, typically in pounds per hour

equipment.name: Steam-to-Hot-Water Converter
equipment.description: Heat exchanger that converts steam heat to hot water for building heating systems

equipment.name: Transfer Fan
equipment.description: Fan used to transfer air from one space to another within a building, typically from a positively pressurized space to a negatively pressurized space.

equipment.name: Uninterruptible Power Supply
equipment.description: Battery-backed power supply that provides immediate backup power during utility outages. Protects critical loads like BAS controllers, servers, and life safety systems.

equipment.name: Unit Heater
equipment.description: Self-contained heating unit with fan, using gas, hot water, or electric heat

equipment.name: Unit Ventilator
equipment.description: Wall-mounted unit providing ventilation and heating/cooling, common in schools and offices

equipment.name: Variable Air Volume Box
equipment.description: Terminal unit that varies airflow to control zone temperature, used in VAV HVAC systems

equipment.name: Variable Frequency Drive
equipment.description: Electronic device that controls motor speed by varying frequency and voltage

equipment.name: VRF Branch Selector Box
equipment.description: Distribution box that directs refrigerant flow to individual indoor units in heat recovery VRF systems

common: variable refrigerant flow branch selector box

synonyms: variable refrigerant flow branch selector box

equipment.name: VRF Indoor Unit
equipment.description: Indoor evaporator unit in VRF system, available in various configurations for zone cooling/heating

equipment.name: VRF Outdoor Unit
equipment.description: Outdoor condensing unit for VRF system, connects to multiple indoor units via refrigerant piping

equipment.name: Water Meter
equipment.description: Device that measures water flow or consumption in gallons or cubic feet

equipment.name: Water Source Heat Pump
equipment.description: Heat pump that exchanges heat with a water loop, often in multi-zone buildings
